```{r}
  # group_by(prolific_subject_id) %>% # can use this and distinct to see different by-subject levels of the counts
 # distinct(prolific_subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/243), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/243), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total)) 



# look at diffs btwn conditions
originality_theme_by_condition <- originality_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  left_join(surveys_df_unique, by = "prolific_subject_id") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      gameplay_mechanics,
      visual_aesthetics,
      theme_narrative_concept,
      audio_sound_music,
      comparative_reference,
      surprise_unexpectedness,
      player_thinking_challenge,
      improve_technical_execution,
      too_confusing_buggy_execution_to_fully_evaluate
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag, condition) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/121), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme, condition) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/121), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total))

wide_originality_theme_by_condition <- originality_theme_by_condition %>%
  select(theme, tag, condition, n, n_pct) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = condition,
    values_from = c(n, n_pct)
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: originality qual on participant level
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false 
# checking whether things were happening on a participant level

originality_theme_counts_by_participant <- originality_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      gameplay_mechanics,
      visual_aesthetics,
      theme_narrative_concept,
      audio_sound_music,
      comparative_reference,
      surprise_unexpectedness,
      player_thinking_challenge,
      improve_technical_execution,
      too_confusing_buggy_execution_to_fully_evaluate
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, prolific_subject_id) %>%
  rename(n_tags_for_subject_theme_valence = n)

# see what percent of the time subjects had a theme appear in more than one of their free responses
originality_theme_counts_by_participant_ <- originality_theme_counts_by_participant %>%
count(n_tags_for_subject_theme_valence) %>%
  mutate(n_prop_sub_repeat_theme = paste0(round(100*(n / sum(n)), 0), "%"))

# if a good chunk of repeats, check which themes (curious if there are dominant themes)  
originality_theme_counts_by_participant_repeats <- originality_theme_counts_by_participant %>%
  filter(n_tags_for_subject_theme_valence > 1)  %>% # run code just to here to see whether it's predominantly 2s or 3s 
  count(theme) %>%
  rename(n_theme_tagged_two_three_times_per_subject = n) %>%
  mutate(prop_sample_repeated_theme = paste0(round(100*(n_theme_tagged_two_three_times_per_subject / sum(n_theme_tagged_two_three_times_per_subject)), 0), "%"))
```

# Qual enjoyability (effectiveness)

```{r}
#| label: effectiveness qual analysis
#| echo: false 

effectiveness_evaluated_qual_data <- read.csv("coded_qual_data/effectiveness_evaluated_data.csv")

effectiveness_theme_counts <- effectiveness_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      mechanics,
      sub_mechanics_execution,
      sub_mechanics_design,
      visuals_aesthetic,
      audio,
      general_no_specification,
      difficult,
      simple,
      confusion,
      bugs,
      concept,
      broken_bugs_couldnt_evaluate_at_all
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  # group_by(prolific_subject_id) %>% # can use this and distinct to see different by-subject levels of the counts
 # distinct(prolific_subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/243), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/243), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total)) 


# look at diffs btwn conditions
effectiveness_theme_by_condition <- effectiveness_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  left_join(surveys_df_unique, by = "prolific_subject_id") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      mechanics,
      sub_mechanics_execution,
      sub_mechanics_design,
      visuals_aesthetic,
      audio,
      general_no_specification,
      difficult,
      simple,
      confusion,
      bugs,
      concept,
      broken_bugs_couldnt_evaluate_at_all
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag, condition) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/121), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme, condition) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/121), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total))

wide_effectiveness_theme_by_condition <- effectiveness_theme_by_condition %>%
  select(theme, tag, condition, n, n_pct) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = condition,
    values_from = c(n, n_pct)
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: effectiveness qual on participant level
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false 
# checking whether things were happening on a participant level

effectiveness_by_participant <- effectiveness_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      mechanics,
      sub_mechanics_execution,
      sub_mechanics_design,
      visuals_aesthetic,
      audio,
      general_no_specification,
      difficult,
      simple,
      confusion,
      bugs,
      concept,
      broken_bugs_couldnt_evaluate_at_all
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, prolific_subject_id) %>% # count number of times each subject tagged each theme positively (tag = 1) and each theme negatively (tag = 2)
  rename(n_tags_for_subject_theme_valence = n)

# see what percent of rows had a theme appear in more than one of their free responses
effectiveness_by_participant_summary <- effectiveness_by_participant %>%
count(n_tags_for_subject_theme_valence) %>%
  mutate(n_prop_sub_repeat_theme = paste0(round(100*(n / sum(n)), 0), "%")) 

# if a good chunk of repeats, check which themes (curious if there are dominant themes)  
effectiveness_theme_counts_by_participant_repeats <- effectiveness_by_participant %>%
  filter(n_tags_for_subject_theme_valence > 1)  %>% # run code just to here to see whether it's predominantly 2s or 3s 
  count(theme) %>% # for each theme, count how many participants mentioned this theme more than once 
  rename(n_participants_tagged_theme_two_three_times = n) %>%
  mutate(prop_sample_repeated_theme = paste0(round(100*(n_participants_tagged_theme_two_three_times / total_num_clean_subjects_in_data), 0), "%"))

```

# Qual authenticity and intentionality

```{r}
#| label: authenticity intentionality qual analysis
#| echo: false 

authint_evaluated_qual_df <- read.csv("coded_qual_data/authenticity_intentionality_evaluated_data.csv")


authint_theme_counts <- authint_evaluated_qual_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      originality,
      overall_idea_concept,
      fun_engagement_challenging,
      visual,
      mechanics_gameplay,
      completeness_execution,
      design_intent,
      personal_invest_effort,
      accessibility,
      ai_gen
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  # group_by(prolific_subject_id) %>% # can use this and distinct to see different by-subject levels of the counts
 # distinct(prolific_subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/243), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/243), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total)) 



# look at diffs btwn conditions
originality_theme_by_condition <- originality_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  left_join(surveys_df_unique, by = "prolific_subject_id") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      gameplay_mechanics,
      visual_aesthetics,
      theme_narrative_concept,
      audio_sound_music,
      comparative_reference,
      surprise_unexpectedness,
      player_thinking_challenge,
      improve_technical_execution,
      too_confusing_buggy_execution_to_fully_evaluate
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag, condition) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/121), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme, condition) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/121), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total))

wide_originality_theme_by_condition <- originality_theme_by_condition %>%
  select(theme, tag, condition, n, n_pct) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = condition,
    values_from = c(n, n_pct)
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: ai aware qual analysis
#| echo: false

ai_aware_evaluated_qual_data <- read.csv("coded_qual_data/ai_aware_evaluated_data.csv")

ai_aware_theme_counts <- ai_aware_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  mutate(valence = case_when(
    valence == "positively_affected" ~ 1,
    valence == "negatively_affected" ~ 2,
    valence == "mixed" ~ 3,
    valence == "no_effect" ~ 4,
    valence == "unclear" ~ 5
      )) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      valence,
      lowered_expectations_standards,
      respect_impressed_AI_capability,
      AI_as_legitimate_tool,
      devaluation_due_to_AI_origin,
      quality_deficits_linked_to_AI,
      human_oversight_needed,
      wasnt_aware_of_ai_generation,
      judged_game_merit,
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  # group_by(prolific_subject_id) %>% # can use this and distinct to see different by-subject levels of the counts
 # distinct(prolific_subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/40), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/40), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total)) 

```

```{r}
#| label: ai blind qual analysis
#| echo: false


ai_blind_evaluated_qual_data <- read.csv("coded_qual_data/ai_blind_evaluated_data.csv")

ai_blind_theme_counts <- ai_blind_evaluated_qual_data %>%
  mutate(valence = case_when(
    valence == "positive" ~ 1,
    valence == "negative" ~ 2,
    valence == "mixed" ~ 3,
    valence == "no_effect" ~ 4,
    valence == "unclear" ~ 5
      )) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(
      valence,
      prior_AI_detection_suspicion,
      judged_game_merit,
      diminished_authenticity,
      reduced_effort_intentionality,
      AI_explains_quality_issues,
      AI_creativity,
      positive_AI_regard
    ),
    names_to = "theme",
    values_to = "tag"
  ) %>%
  # group_by(prolific_subject_id) %>% # can use this and distinct to see different by-subject levels of the counts
 # distinct(prolific_subject_id, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  filter(tag > 0) %>%
  count(theme, tag) %>%
  mutate(n_pct = paste0(round(100*(n/41), 0), "%")) %>% # turn into percent for easy reporting 
  group_by(theme) %>%
  mutate(n_total = sum(n), # grab total sum
         n_total_pct = paste0(round(100*(n_total/41), 0), "%")) %>%# turn into percent for easy reporting
  arrange(desc(n_total)) 

```

# Game concept

```{r}
#| label: game concept analyses (RQ3)
#| echo: false

# analyze concept creativity and implementation accuracy using the LMM framework
concept_creativity_lmm <- lmer(concept_creativity ~ condition + (1 | game_id) + (1 | prolific_subject_id), data = clean_analysis_df)
implementation_accuracy_lmm <- lmer(implementation_accuracy ~ condition + (1 | game_id) + (1 | prolific_subject_id), data = clean_analysis_df)

# marginal means for concept creativity and implementation accuracy
concept_creativity_means <- emmeans(concept_creativity_lmm, ~ condition) %>% as.data.frame()
implementation_accuracy_means <- emmeans(implementation_accuracy_lmm, ~ condition) %>% as.data.frame()

# comparison: Concept Creativity vs. Game Creativity (within-participant)
# pivot long for Rating Type (Concept vs. Game)
long_comparison_df <- clean_analysis_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(creativity, concept_creativity),
    names_to = "rating_type",
    values_to = "creativity_rating"
  ) %>%
  mutate(rating_type = factor(rating_type, levels = c("concept_creativity", "creativity")))

# repeated-measures LMM with within-participant factor of Rating Type
rating_type_comparison_lmm <- lmer(
  creativity_rating ~ rating_type * condition + (1 | game_id) + (1 | prolific_subject_id),
  data = long_comparison_df
)

# interaction: Rating Type x Condition
rating_type_means <- emmeans(rating_type_comparison_lmm, ~ rating_type * condition) %>% as.data.frame()

# exploratory moderation: Does implementation accuracy affect the relationship between concept creativity and game creativity?
moderation_lmm <- lmer(
  creativity ~ concept_creativity * implementation_accuracy + (1 | game_id) + (1 | prolific_subject_id),
  data = clean_analysis_df
)


# look at results 
#summary(concept_creativity_lmm)
#summary(implementation_accuracy_lmm)
#concept_creativity_means
#implementation_accuracy_means

#summary(rating_type_comparison_lmm)
#rating_type_means

# main effect of rating type
#emmeans(rating_type_comparison_lmm, pairwise ~ rating_type)

# interaction breakdown
#emmeans(rating_type_comparison_lmm, pairwise ~ rating_type | condition)

# look at whether implementation moderates concept to real game ratings 
#summary(moderation_lmm)

# probe the interaction: simple slopes of concept_creativity 
# at low, medium, and high implementation accuracy
#emtrends(moderation_lmm, ~ implementation_accuracy, 
 #        var = "concept_creativity",
  #       at = list(implementation_accuracy = c(1, 3, 5)))
```
